headline: Who funds George Chen's campaign?
dek: Donor data for the mayoral race, comparing George Chen and Sharon Kalani.

geo.heading: Where do the donors live?
geo.sub: 45% of Chen's donors are Torrance residents. 85% of Kalani's are.

stats.heading: Donors at the legal maximum
stats.sub: Torrance caps individual donations at $1,000 per election. Percentage of each candidate's maxed-out donors who are from outside Torrance:

cards.label: Chen's maxed-out donors from outside Torrance
Kalani's maxed-out donors from outside Torrance

occupation.heading: Where do the donor dollars come from?
occupation.sub: Each candidate's donor dollars grouped by occupation as reported on their filings.<sup>1</sup>

contrast.heading: Occupation comparison
contrast.sub: The two occupation groups from the chart above shown side by side: business owners, professionals, and their family<sup>2</sup> vs. teachers, public servants, and service workers.

labels:
Business owners, professionals,
and their family

callouts.title: 8% of Chen's donor dollars from shared-surname donors
callouts.body: 23 Chen donors filed as not employed and share a last name with at least one other Chen donor.<sup>2</sup> Torrance limits individual donations to $1,000. Kalani has zero donors in this category.

callouts.title: Out-of-state donors
callouts.body: Chen received contributions from donors in Maryland, Texas, Ohio, and Nevada. Kalani's filings show none from out of state.

callouts.title: Real estate industry donors
callouts.body: Real estate and property professionals contributed to Chen's campaign. Donors include Aurelio Mattucci, a real estate broker who is also running for City Treasurer.

callouts.title: Average donation by geography
callouts.body: Chen's non-Torrance donors averaged 24% more per person than his Torrance-based donors.

footnotes.body: "Business owners, professionals, and their family" includes donors with filed titles of: business owner, president, CEO, CFO, founder, executive, engineer, programmer, physician, doctor, attorney, real estate broker/agent/developer, property investor, financial planner, finance manager, CPA, architect, director, manager, consultant, pharmacist, banker, insurance agent, and accountant -- as well as family members (see note 2). "Teachers, public servants, and service workers" includes: teacher, professor, librarian, police officer, mayor, mechanic, bank teller, pastoral assistant, sales, instructor, and similar titles. "Retired" and "not employed" are reported as filed. Two Chen donors (under 1% of dollars) did not fit any category and are excluded.

"Their family" identifies donors who (a) filed a job title of unemployed, housewife, or homemaker and (b) share a last name with at least one other donor to the same candidate. Shared last names do not prove a family relationship -- common surnames may produce false matches. This grouping surfaces a pattern, not an assertion of specific relationships.
All data from $100+ contributions filed with the City of Torrance through December 31, 2025 via Torrance NetFile. Contributions under $100 are not required to be itemized and are not included.
